summary: R3 fixes the remaining QA R2 visible localization issue. The active district sort indicator no longer renders literal English "up" or "down" in default player mode; visible sort direction text and active sort accessible labels now come from packages/ui/src/i18n.ts. Focused zh-CN/system fallback coverage asserts the active sort button renders localized visible text such as "地区 升序" and fails if English up/down returns.

tests_run.result: On feat/m7-app-shell-quality; R3 working tree changes are limited to packages/ui i18n/shell, web shell tests, e2e test, and this handoff.
No matches for the prior literal English sort suffixes in SortButton implementation.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M7-APP-SHELL-QUALITY-001

tests_run.result: Vitest web shell suite passed: 1 test file, 9 tests, including zh-CN active sort visible suffix regression.
Vitest UI suite passed: 1 test file, 15 tests.
Playwright Chromium suite passed: 15 tests, including system zh-CN fallback active sort visible text.
Format, lint, recursive typecheck, and architecture boundary checks passed.

decisions.decision: Used i18n catalog keys for active sort direction labels instead of locale-neutral icons so both visible text and accessible state remain explicit in en-US and zh-CN.

Limited R3 to the QA R2 sort-direction localization issue; no domain rules, protocol, Electron IPC, release, branding, art, telemetry, server, multiplayer, account, cloud save, or manual battle scope changed.
Sort UI still only coordinates the existing client read-model list projection and local presentation state; it does not own WorldState or mutate authoritative simulation state.

risks.summary: Active sort buttons now expose localized state in their aria-label, for example 'Sort by District, currently ascending' or '按地区排序，当前升序'. Inactive sort labels remain unchanged.

requested_action: Please independently revalidate M7-APP-SHELL-QUALITY-001 R3, focusing on the QA R2 finding that default player-mode active sort direction must not show English up/down in zh-CN/system fallback and must route through the accepted i18n foundation.
